service.description: API Management appears across 50 normalized findings in 3 checklist families. 1 GA-ready families can anchor the baseline. 2 lower-confidence families broaden design coverage.
service.whatThisMeans: Start with the GA-ready families for this service, then widen into preview guidance only when the review question requires more depth.

quality.label: GA · High confidence
quality.summary: Strong review accelerator. Quality score 93 with 83% metadata completeness and 100% severity coverage.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

items.text: Deploy a API Gateway solution like API-Management to load balance requests, rate limit tokens, keyless authentication and monitor AI usage.
Require clients to authenticate using Entra ID when accessing AI model endpoints.
Azure API Management platform version should be stv2
items.description: Upgrading to API Management stv2 is required as stv1 retires on 31 Aug 2024, offering enhanced capabilities with the new platform version.
items.graph:
// Azure Resource Graph Query
// Find all API Management instances that aren't upgraded to platform version stv2
resources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.ApiManagement/service'
| extend plat_version = properties.platformVersion
| extend skuName = sku.name
| where tolower(plat_version) != tolower('stv2')
| project recommendationId = "e35cf148-8eee-49d1-a1c9-956160f99e0b", name, id, tags, param1=strcat("Platform Version: ", plat_version) , param2=strcat("SKU: ", skuName)

items.text: Enable Availability Zones on Premium API Management instances
items.description: Zone redundancy for APIM instances ensures the gateway and control plane (Management API, developer portal, Git configuration) are replicated across datacenters in physically separated zones, boosting resilience to zone failures.
items.graph:
// Azure Resource Graph Query
// Find all Premium API Management instances that aren't zone redundant
resources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.ApiManagement/service'
| extend skuName = sku.name
| where tolower(skuName) == tolower('premium')
| where isnull(zones) or array_length(zones) < 2
| extend zoneValue = iff((isnull(zones)), "null", zones)
| project recommendationId = "740f2c1c-8857-4648-80eb-47d2c56d5a50", name, id, tags, param1="Zones: No Zone or Zonal", param2=strcat("Zones value: ", zoneValue )

items.text: Migrate API Management services to Premium SKU to support Availability Zones
items.description: Upgrading the API Management instance to the Premium SKU adds support for Availability Zones, enhancing availability and resilience by distributing services across physically separate locations within Azure regions.
items.graph:
// Azure Resource Graph Query
// Find all API Management instances that aren't Premium
resources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.ApiManagement/service'
| extend skuName = sku.name
| where tolower(skuName) != tolower('premium')
| project recommendationId = "baf3bfc0-32a2-4c0c-926d-c9bf0b49808e", name, id, tags, param1=strcat("SKU: ", skuName)

items.text: Be aware of APIM's limits
Configure alerts on the most critical metrics
Disable Public Network Access
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = (properties.virtualNetworkType == 'Internal') | distinct id, compliant

items.text: Enable Diagnostics Settings to export logs to Azure Monitor
Ensure that custom SSL certificates are stored an Azure Key Vault so they can be securely accessed and updated
Ensure that secrets (Named values) are stored an Azure Key Vault so they can be securely accessed and updated
Ensure that the self-hosted gateway deployments are resilient.
Ensure there is an automated backup routine
Protect incoming requests to APIs (data plane) with Azure AD
Upgrade the platform version and follow lifecyle. stv1 is retirng on 31 August 2024
items.graph: resources | where type =~ 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = (properties.platformVersion != 'stv1') | project id, compliant

items.text: Use the latest TLS version when encrypting information in transit. Disable outdated and unnecessary protocols and ciphers when possible.
Use web application firewall (WAF) by deploying Application Gateway in front of APIM
Enforce recommended guardrails for API Management
Apply throttling policies to control the number of requests per second
Configure APIM via Infrastructure-as-code. Review DevOps best practices from the Cloud Adoption Framework APIM Landing Zone Accelerator
Configure autoscaling to scale out the number of instances when the load increases
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | join kind = leftouter (resources | where type == 'microsoft.insights/autoscalesettings' | extend targetResourceUri = tostring(properties.targetResourceUri)) on $left.id == $right.targetResourceUri | extend compliant = (sku.name == 'Premium' and isnotempty(targetResourceUri) and properties1.enabled == true) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: Create appropriate groups to control the visibility of the products
Deploy at least one unit in two or more availability zones for an increased SLA of 99.99%
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = ( sku.name == 'Premium' and  isnotnull(zones) and sku.capacity >= 2 ) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: Deploy network security groups (NSG) to your subnets to restrict or monitor traffic to/from APIM.
Deploy Private Endpoints to filter incoming traffic when APIM is not deployed to a VNet.
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = (properties.virtualNetworkType == 'None' and isnotnull(properties.privateEndpointConnections)) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: Deploy self-hosted gateways where Azure doesn't have a region close to the backend APIs.
Deploy the service within a Virtual Network (VNet)
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(properties.virtualNetworkConfiguration)) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: Enable Application Insights for more detailed telemetry
Ensure all APIs policies include a <base/> element.
For DR, leverage the premium tier with deployments scaled across two or more regions for 99.99% SLA
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = ( sku.name == 'Premium' and  isnotnull(properties.additionalLocations)) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: If you are planning to monetize your APIs, review the 'monetization support' article for best practices
Implement an error handling policy at the global level
Implement DevOps and CI/CD in your workflow
In multi-region model, use Policies to route the requests to regional backends based on availability or latency.
Promote usage of Visual Studio Code APIM extension for faster API development
Review 'Recommendations to mitigate OWASP API Security Top 10 threats' article and check what is applicable to your APIs
Secure APIs using client certificate authentication
Secure backend services using client certificate authentication
Simplify management with PowerShell automation scripts
Use Authorizations feature to simplify management of OAuth 2.0 token for your backend APIs
Use Azure Front Door in front of APIM for multi-region deployment
Use Backends feature to eliminate redundant API backend configurations
Use managed identities to authenticate to other Azure resources whenever possible
items.graph: resources | where type == 'microsoft.apimanagement/service' | extend compliant = (isnotnull(identity)) | distinct id, compliant

items.text: Use Microsoft Entra ID to authenticate users in the Developer Portal
Use Named Values to store common values that can be used in policies
Use Policies to add a fail-over backend URL and caching to reduce failing calls.
Use Policy Fragments to avoid repeating same policies definitions across multiple APIs
Use the premium tier for production workloads.
Enable auto-scale for production workloads on API Management services
items.description: Use API Management with auto-scale for high availability in workloads that experience variable traffic patterns. There are several limitations with auto-scale, so review the documentation to ensure it meets your requirements.

items.text: If you need to log at high performance levels, consider Event Hubs policy
